thought: Blend the first-frame battle composition with the scraped gameplay video's mobile-promo motion language: keep the exact scene layout, character placement, camera angle, UI framing, and color logic from 0045.jpg as the perception lock, then animate it with the reference video's snappy vertical-game-ad timing, elastic UI pops, fast readable hit beats, light screen shake, punchy smear-like motion, bright impact flashes, and scrapbook/sticker-style VFX. The action should feel like a polished in-game ultimate showcase based on real gameplay pacing: brief anticipation, rapid power-up, explosive forward attack, layered hit confirms, damage-number/UI-style accents, and a clean heroic settle. Do not replace the scene design with the reference video's characters; only inherit its motion rhythm, camera punch-ins, mobile-readable staging, and casual promo-style VFX cadence.
prompt_en:
First Frame: @0045.jpg. Reference Video: @taptap_xundaodaqian_杖剑传说_实机_1774616335.mp4. Motion Style Reference: adopt the scraped gameplay video's vertical mobile promo timing, snappy elastic 2D-game UI motion, readable impact staging, quick punch-in camera beats, playful but forceful hit VFX, and polished real-game ultimate pacing, while strictly preserving the first frame's scene composition, character identity, environment, and battle layout.
[00s-01s] Hard lock to the exact composition of @0045.jpg. Start with a very short hold for readability, then subtle ambient motion only: tiny energy flicker, cloth/hair drift, idle particles, faint glow pulsing around the hero. Camera remains mostly locked to first-frame framing.
[01s-02s] Ultimate anticipation phase. The hero lowers center of gravity or draws power inward based on the pose in the first frame. Add tight energy gathering around weapon/body, circular rune/light streaks, rising dust, and a soft vignette. Use the reference video's snappy easing: quick scale pop, slight camera push-in, elastic recoil. UI/gameplay readability first.
[02s-03s] Power-up spike. Bright layered aura expands outward in 2-3 pulses, with sticker-like impact accents and short radial lines inspired by the reference video's promo language. Add micro screen shake and brief exposure bloom. Enemy reacts slightly to pressure wave. Maintain the exact environment and battle lane from the first frame.
[03s-04s] Dash or launch into the ultimate attack. Fast forward burst with motion trails from the hero, smear-like directional streaks, and a sharp camera punch following the attack vector. Timing should match gameplay showcase rhythm: extremely readable, not chaotic. The move should feel derived from real in-game demo footage rather than cinematic realism.
[04s-05s] Main impact beat. Large hit flash, layered slash/energy burst depending on the hero's weapon style, strong but short screen shake, hit-stop for a few frames, then release. Add floating damage-number style accents or UI-like celebratory burst elements in the same casual mobile-game promo spirit, but integrated into the battle scene. Enemy is struck hard with a visible recoil arc.
[05s-06s] Combo continuation. 2 to 3 rapid follow-up hit confirmations with rhythmic cadence from the reference video: each hit has a clean anticipation-contact-recoil cycle, bright outlined VFX, and quick camera nudges. Keep the scene readable on a mobile screen; avoid over-dense effects blocking the character silhouettes.
[06s-07s] Finisher climax. A larger signature effect fills the center of frame: energy column, crescent slash, rune burst, or explosive magical emblem matching the hero design from the first frame and sprite sheet. Use a short dramatic pause before the final blow, then a decisive impact with strongest bloom and debris/particle burst.
[07s-08s] Resolution. Effects disperse into sparkles, smoke wisps, and fading light fragments. Enemy remains defeated, staggered, or knocked back according to the battle logic implied by the first frame. Camera eases back slightly from the punch-in. Hero returns to a strong victory or post-ultimate stance.
[08s-09s] End card style hold within gameplay scene. Preserve the battle environment and final pose, with lingering glow, drifting particles, and minimal idle animation. The final frame should feel like a premium gameplay promo freeze-frame, clean and readable for vertical social/mobile presentation.
Visual rules: preserve first-frame composition and art direction exactly; use the reference video only for motion timing, camera punch style, hit rhythm, elastic UI-like physics, and VFX cadence. Bright colorful fantasy combat, polished mobile-game ad feel, crisp silhouette separation, no character substitution, no scene redesign, no photorealism.
prompt_zh:
> 首帧锁定: @0045.jpg。参考视频: @taptap_xundaodaqian_杖剑传说_实机_1774616335.mp4。动作风格参考: 采用参考实机视频中的竖屏手游宣传动效节奏、清晰直接的战斗演出、带弹性的UI式运动、快速有力的镜头推拉、轻巧但有冲击力的受击反馈与特效层次，同时严格保留首帧中的角色设定、场景构图、镜头角度、战斗站位与整体美术风格不变。
[00s-01s] 严格锁定@0045.jpg的初始构图，先短暂停留保证识别度。只做轻微待机动态：角色衣摆/头发轻晃、能量微闪、环境粒子漂浮、武器或身体周围有淡淡发光呼吸感。镜头基本不动。
[01s-02s] 进入大招前摇蓄力。角色根据首帧姿态进行重心压低或能量回收动作，身体/武器周围快速聚集光效、符文环、风压和地面尘屑。参考视频的节奏做一个轻微镜头前推，并带一点弹性回弹，突出手游宣传片式的干净利落。
[02s-03s] 蓄力爆发。能量光环以2到3段脉冲向外扩张，加入类似贴纸感、描边感的冲击装饰线和高亮爆点，体现参考视频的轻快夸张UI动效语言。配合微弱屏幕震动和短暂曝光提升，敌人受到气压震慑产生小幅后仰。
[03s-04s] 角色释放大招本体，向前突进或跃击。动作需要像实机演示那样清晰、迅速、可读，不走电影写实路线。增加方向性拖尾、速度线、残影，以及跟随攻击方向的快速镜头冲击，但仍保持首帧战场空间关系不变。
[04s-05s] 第一段主命中。出现大面积高亮受击闪光、武器斩击或魔法爆裂核心特效，加入短暂顿帧感(hit-stop)后再释放冲击，屏幕震动强但时间短。可叠加轻量的数字伤害感/UI庆祝式爆点元素，借鉴参考视频的手游广告表现方式，但不要遮挡主体轮廓。敌人明显被重击击退。
[05s-06s] 连段追击。连续2到3次节奏明确的补刀命中，每一下都遵循“起手-接触-后坐”的清楚节拍，镜头做小幅跟震，特效采用亮色描边、清晰层次和卡通化能量爆点。保证手机端观看时角色与敌人的剪影始终清楚。
[06s-07s] 终结技高潮。释放更大的标志性终结特效，可为能量柱、弧月斩、魔法阵爆发、符文印记炸裂等，具体形式需贴合首帧角色与已有素材设定。先有极短蓄势停顿，再打出最强一击，伴随最强辉光、粒子喷发和地面/空气冲击波。
[07s-08s] 收招结算。强特效逐渐散成火花、烟雾、碎光和残余能量丝带，敌人进入被击倒、重创僵直或被击飞后的落势状态。镜头从近处稍微回收，角色落回稳定姿态，形成大招收尾英雄感。
[08s-09s] 结尾定格。保留战斗场景和角色终结后站姿，环境中残留少量漂浮粒子、余辉和能量尾迹，形成像高品质手游实机宣传片一样的干净收尾画面，适合竖屏短视频停帧展示。
规则要求：必须严格继承首帧构图、角色、场景、美术风格与战斗布局；参考视频只用于动作节奏、镜头推拉、受击物理、UI式弹性动效和特效演出节拍。整体呈现为明亮、利落、夸张但清晰的奇幻大招战斗演示，不替换角色，不改场景，不做写实电影化。
